_meta.description: Canonical CV data for David Pérez Arbués. All fields are in English.
_meta.link_syntax: Use [display text](dest) anywhere inside a string value. Two destination types are supported: external URLs (https://...) and internal PDF jumps (#section-id). Internal links navigate to a section heading within the same PDF.
link_examples: Internal jump:  "See the [Bolder Award](#awards) for the recognition"

summary: Engineering leader with 15+ years of experience, currently Engineering Lead within adidas DXP managing a 12-engineer team (5 direct reports) across Membership and Acquisition. Known for building high-performing teams and operating above role — including covering the Director of Software Engineering position and co-designing the 2026 value-led team structure [Leadership Profile](#leadership_profile). Combines strong people leadership with deep hands-on technical expertise: recently pioneered AI-enabled delivery at scale, cutting a 6-month project to 3 months [Bolder Award](#awards).

leadership_profile.subtitle: 12 engineers (9 Membership + 3 Acquisition), 5 direct reports
highlights: Covered Director of Software Engineering role for 1+ month during leave — operated at M2 level, participated in Senior Director meetings, owned cross-team reporting and co-designed the 2026 value-led team structure (cross-functional squads: backend, .com, iOS, Android)

Onboarded and integrated 3 new direct reports in 2025, creating a supportive and encouraging environment that fosters growth; managed complex personnel dynamics while keeping the team focused and motivated
Direct report feedback rated 'among the best I have seen' by manager across career; peers describe leadership as creating 'a safe and motivating environment where every voice is heard'
Consistently rated ALMOST ALWAYS (4.0/5) on people management across four consecutive review cycles (2024–2025)

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer → Engineering Lead, .com Membership · adidas DXP
experience.subtitle: adidas · Zaragoza, Spain
highlights: 2025 — Covered the Director of Software Engineering role during a period of leave: participated in Director-level meetings with the Senior Director of SE, reported across all of Membership (.com + apps), and collaborated on the 2026 value-led team structure (cross-functional squads: backend, .com, iOS, Android)

Led .com Membership team (9 engineers, 5 direct reports) with expanded scope covering .com Acquisition (3 engineers, Glass decommission); responsible for team KPIs, hiring, capacity planning, and coaching — including security issue resolution and collaboration with QA Excellence on testing standards

2026 — AI-enabled engineering transformation: pioneered GenAI-assisted delivery for the Glass Decommissioning initiative, delivering in 3 months what was estimated at 6 months with 4.5 FTEs — recognised with the adidas Bolder Award (April 2026); learnings shared across DXP and CE to drive broader adoption
Led go-live security process for Glass Decommissioning: owned security architecture review, coordinated penetration testing, and resolved all findings before production release

Upper Funnel platform: drove Latte cross-platform initiative, expanding JSON-driven rendering to PDP, PLP, and navigation surfaces — owning the product discovery and presentation layer architecture across web and mobile; coordinated joint API contracts and releases across .com and iOS/Android teams

Internalized a fully external team (12 Merkle contractors → in-house) in 7 months; delivered the resulting microfrontend migration in 3 months on a hard deadline, extending the framework to support nested microfrontends
Defined and owned testing strategy: coverage thresholds, rotating quality champion role, Sonar gates, githooks, and improved deployment flows — lifting quality culture across both teams

experience.title: Tech Lead & Sole Engineer
experience.subtitle: Partum · Remote
highlights: Designed and built a new generation smart locker kiosk from scratch — edge-first, Raspberry Pi + NestJS + React inside Docker managed by Balena
Integrated Kerong smart lock hardware via a custom TCP binary protocol (STX/ETX framed) over raw sockets
Kiosk UI driven entirely by XState v5 state machines (delivery and pickup flows)
Moved cloud communication to AWS IoT and WebSockets; provisioned all infrastructure with AWS CDK v2

experience.title: Software Engineer (Backend Lead, Connection Studio)
experience.subtitle: adidas · Zaragoza, Spain
highlights: Led the full re-architecture of Connection Studio 2.0: migrated from Java/Spring to Node.js/NestJS/TypeScript, replaced Oracle DB with DynamoDB — serving global adidas.com login and campaign traffic at scale

Built a suite of internal NestJS libraries before equivalent open-source solutions existed: fluent DynamoDB query builder, decorator-driven Kafka router, @Cache() method decorator, runtime decorator discovery
Reduced monthly deployments to weekly via GitHub Flow and CI/CD improvements; introduced Sonar, Instana, Grafana, Opsgenie
Migrated mission-critical microservices to AWS Lambda; introduced Terraform and AWS CDK for IaC
Security Champion: coordinated security architecture reviews and penetration testing for go-live; led security practices across the team throughout the re-architecture
Mentored one engineer across 3+ years and 3 technology shifts (Java BE → Node.js BE → React FE)
Promoted to Senior Software Engineer in 2022

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.subtitle: GFT Group · Zaragoza, Spain (client: Deutsche Bank UK)
highlights: Technical area lead for architecture, code quality, and security on the EUS project at Deutsche Bank UK
80% reduction in on-call incidents through Java and Unix quality engineering
Led international knowledge transfer to HCL India (technical and functional, delivered in English); led disaster recovery planning across geographies
Voluntarily took on frontend work on dbPalace after self-studying Angular when no frontend engineer was available

experience.title: Consultant / Analyst
experience.subtitle: everis · Madrid, Spain (client: Ministerio de Economía y Competitividad)
highlights: Developer and analyst for the unified application processing system and its data model
Continuous development and improvement of the document processing system
Created and improved Oracle stored procedures for inter-application data transfers

experience.title: Researcher
experience.subtitle: Universidad de Zaragoza · Zaragoza, Spain
highlights: Research on Deep Belief Networks applied to brain-computer interfaces: using EEG signals to control a wheelchair through pattern recognition
Implemented learning algorithms in Matlab; built a handwritten digit recogniser via camera as proof of concept
[Publication](https://zaguan.unizar.es/record/5100)

education.title: Universidad de Zaragoza
education.subtitle: Ingeniería Superior de Telecomunicaciones · Master's equivalent (EQF Level 7)
education.description: Covers networks, systems, signal processing, and embedded systems. Combined with part-time work during the later years.

certifications.title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure 2023 Certified Architect Associate
certifications.subtitle: Oracle · 2023

certifications.title: Cambridge English: Advanced (CAE) — Grade A — C2
certifications.subtitle: University of Cambridge · 2016

skills.name: Cloud & Infrastructure
entries: AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, S3, CDK, IoT)

awards.title: Bolder Award — AI Native Guild
awards.subtitle: adidas · April 2026
awards.description: Selected by the M1 panel for the inaugural Bolder Awards for leading the Glass Decommissioning initiative. Estimated at 6 months with 4.5 FTEs — delivered in 3 months including unplanned scope, by pioneering GenAI-assisted component re-implementation. Built an AI-powered SDLC orchestration platform (Claude Agent SDK + XState v5 + Next.js) that drives tickets autonomously through analysis → implementation → code review → security audit → PR. Learnings shared across DXP and CE teams to drive broader AI adoption.

side_projects.title: Latte template AI analysis pipeline
side_projects.subtitle: In progress
side_projects.description: Using AI to build agents, commands, and tools that reduce tech debt across Latte templates — surfacing and fixing redundant state patterns, dead content, and hardcoded strings, while driving harmonisation across templates. Goal: lower the cost of creating new templates for markets, reduce the maintenance burden on existing ones, and reduce the risk of production issues caused by misconfiguration.

side_projects.title: AI homelab sandbox
side_projects.subtitle: Active
side_projects.description: Proxmox NAS running Ollama, n8n, and Claude in a sandboxed environment for testing AI integrations before bringing them to production. Used to explore advisor patterns, token reduction strategies, prompt injection hardening, and agentic workflow design.

side_projects.title: Home automation & IoT fleet
side_projects.subtitle: Active
side_projects.description: Declarative management of OpenBeken/Tuya smart devices via Ansible and Home Assistant. Being replaced by obk-fleet — a Go daemon using the Kubernetes operator pattern (desired state, reconciliation loop, drift detection) for full fleet management.

side_projects.title: cv-web
side_projects.subtitle: Active
side_projects.description: The tool used to generate this CV. Typst-powered editor with drag-and-drop layout, live PDF preview, multi-template support, and per-section spacing controls. Sections are data-driven and dynamically discovered from cv.json — layouts are portable JSON configs that can be saved, imported, and shared.

side_projects.title: Technical writing
side_projects.subtitle: In progress
side_projects.description: Article series on AI for engineers — 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly' — covering practical AI adoption, honest trade-offs, and engineering perspective on LLMs. Published on Medium and LinkedIn.
